title: Memory dashboard: visual surface for tiers, live hybrid retrieval scores, and the forgetting ledger (judge-facing demo view)
source: hackathon gap analysis 2026-07-17 ('your demo surface is a CLI; competitors' demos are visual')
current_state: Pi's strongest Track-1 assets are invisible: the three-tier memory state lives in SQLite, retrieve()'s fused dense+BM25 scores are computed then discarded after ranking, and the forgetting ledger (T-301) is CLI-only (scripts/memory_cli.py cmd_forgotten). The brain server (app/server.py, T-187) serves only a chat UI at GET /. A judge watching the demo video sees terminal text; nothing shows memory filling, retrieval scoring, or facts dying. Additionally the T-301 classification logic (EXPIRED/CONTRADICTED/MERGED precedence) lives inline in cmd_forgotten - a server endpoint duplicating it would recreate the write/read divergence class.
target_state: GET /memory serves a dark single-page dashboard (web/memory.html, same styling family as web/index.html, plain JS, no new deps) with three panels: (1) memory state - per-tier row counts + top-N hot L3 rows with importance/pinned/category; (2) live retrieval - a query box that calls retrieve() and renders each hit with its fused score; (3) the forgetting ledger - recent EXPIRED/CONTRADICTED/MERGED rows with reason badges. Backing endpoints GET /memory/state, /memory/retrieve?q=, /memory/forgotten (all read-only, all behind the existing _verify_token dependency). The ledger classification moves to ONE shared implementation: MemoryTools.forgotten_ledger(days) in tools_memory.py; cmd_forgotten refactors to call it with byte-identical JSON output (existing test_memory_cli.py forgotten tests must stay green unmodified - they are the contract).
migration_plan: 1. Extract cmd_forgotten's classification into MemoryTools.forgotten_ledger(days=7) -> list[dict] (same keys: id/content/importance/category/reason/when/pointer_id/superseded_by_snippet; same precedence: invalid_at beats expired beats merged; MERGED always included - no timestamp to window on).
2. Refactor scripts/memory_cli.py cmd_forgotten to call it; keep text rendering + counts footer untouched. Existing forgotten tests in test_memory_cli.py pass UNMODIFIED.
3. app/server.py: three GET endpoints behind _verify_token; /memory serves web/memory.html (mirror the GET / pattern).
4. web/memory.html: three panels, fetch with the web/chat.js token pattern (Bearer from localStorage), auto-refresh state+ledger every 10s, retrieval on submit.

5. Tests in test_brain_server.py's TestClient pattern: state/forgotten/retrieve endpoints return expected shapes from a mocked agent.memory; auth enforced on all three (401 with token set, 200 with correct bearer); /memory serves HTML.
6. Ledger-reuse test: MemoryTools.forgotten_ledger against a seeded temp DB classifies all three reasons (port the seed fixture from test_memory_cli.py).
7. docs/DEMO_SCRIPT.md: point Beat 2/3 camera shots at the dashboard.
risk_notes: Read-only endpoints over existing data - zero write-path risk. The refactor in step 1-2 is the only regression surface: test_memory_cli.py's forgotten tests are the byte-level contract, run them before and after. retrieve() on the endpoint is best-effort: no embedding provider configured degrades to BM25 exactly like everywhere else (no special handling). Keep the page dependency-free (no CDN - the demo box may have restricted egress and the page must work offline).
